evals.prompt: I have a Claude Code plugin in a private GitHub repo with a marketplace.json already set up. I want to publish it on SkillStack as a free plugin. The marketplace.json has one plugin called 'code-helper' at version 1.0.0.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads marketplace.json, confirms free pricing, writes skillstack.json with storefront URL, guides through GitHub App install, commits and pushes, verifies registration
expectations: Reads .claude-plugin/marketplace.json as the first step
Asks about pricing model (free vs paid)
For free plugins, does NOT require payment provider configuration
Derives GitHub org from git remote URL
Derives storefront URL using the marketplace name slug
Guides user to install SkillStack Distribution GitHub App
Asks for permission before running git commit and push
Calls skillstack_list MCP tool to verify registration after push
Shows a summary with storefront URL and buyer quick-start instructions

evals.prompt: I want to publish my plugin as a paid plugin using Polar with a subscription license model. My Polar org ID is 0c504f49-dbdd-496a-8a36-72ce2a94d97f and product ID is 11297490-6b02-4468-8968-1813b187343d. The plugin is called 'analytics-pro' at version 2.0.0.
evals.expected_output: Skill collects Polar credentials, validates UUID format, writes skillstack.json with license_provider and license_config, offers free tier and creator contact
expectations: Asks which payment provider (Polar or Lemon Squeezy)
Asks about single vs multi-license type
Collects Polar org_id in UUID format
Collects Polar product_id in UUID format
Asks about License Key benefit configuration
Writes config to .claude-plugin/skillstack.json (NOT marketplace.json)
Config includes license_provider: polar and license_model: subscription
Offers optional free tier (freemium) configuration
Offers optional creator_contact configuration

evals.prompt: I have 3 plugins in my marketplace. Two are already on SkillStack (linear-pm as subscription, code-reviewer as lifetime). I want to reconfigure linear-pm to offer both subscription and lifetime options (multi-license).
evals.expected_output: Skill detects already-connected plugins, offers reconfigure option, handles multi-license flow with per-type benefit IDs
expectations: Shows which plugins are already connected vs not connected
Offers reconfigure licensing option when any plugins are already connected
Handles multi-license flow requiring at least 2 license types
For multi-license with Polar, collects a separate benefit_id per license type
Uses license_options instead of license_model for multi-license
Does NOT include license_model when using license_options

evals.prompt: I want to publish my plugin with Lemon Squeezy. It should be a paid plugin with a lifetime license and a free tier. My store ID is 306756, product ID is 12345. I want to offer 3 skills for free out of 8 total.
evals.expected_output: Skill collects LS credentials, configures freemium with free_skills array, writes skillstack.json with complete config
expectations: Collects Lemon Squeezy store_id
Collects Lemon Squeezy product_id
Asks about free tier after license configuration
Lists all skill directories from skills/ folder for free tier selection
Stores selected free skills in a free_skills array
Warns if all skills are selected as free
Confirms the free/premium split before proceeding

evals.prompt: My marketplace.json still has old SkillStack fields like license_provider and license_config from a prior publish. I want to publish a new plugin to SkillStack.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects and cleans stale SkillStack fields from marketplace.json during the publish flow
expectations: Detects stale SkillStack fields in marketplace.json during the publish flow
Strips license_provider, license_config, license_model, and other SkillStack fields from marketplace.json
Shows the creator what fields were cleaned up
Does NOT modify non-SkillStack fields in marketplace.json
Stages both skillstack.json and marketplace.json for the git commit
